notes.content: All transfer applicants must meet the University of California admissions eligibility requirements and the campus’s admission selection criteria. All requirements must be completed by the end of the spring term prior to transfer. For more information on UC admissions eligibility and admission to UC Santa Cruz, visit the Admissions website . The UC Santa Cruz Transfer Admission Guarantee (TAG) is a formal agreement that guarantees your fall admission to your chosen major, provided you are transferring from a California Community College and meet specific requirements. Visit the UC Santa Cruz TAG webpage for more information.
notes.contentHtml:
All transfer applicants must meet the University of California admissions eligibility requirements and the campus’s admission selection criteria. All requirements must be completed by the end of the spring term prior to transfer. For more information on UC admissions eligibility and admission to UC Santa Cruz, visit the Admissions website.


The UC Santa Cruz Transfer Admission Guarantee (TAG) is a formal agreement that guarantees your fall admission to your chosen major, provided you are transferring from a California Community College and meet specific requirements. Visit the UC Santa Cruz TAG webpage for more information.

notes.content: This is a non-screening major: completion of specific major coursework is not required for admission. However, students are strongly encouraged to complete as many lower-division courses as possible from the " Recommended Preparation " section below to support timely degree completion. Students applying to non-screening majors are also encouraged to complete the California General Education Transfer Curriculum (Cal-GETC) or all UC Santa Cruz general education requirements prior to transfer. For guidance on incorporating Cal-GETC into your academic plan, consult your community college counselor.
notes.contentHtml:
This is a non-screening major: completion of specific major coursework is not required for admission. However, students are strongly encouraged to complete as many lower-division courses as possible from the "Recommended Preparation" section below to support timely degree completion.


Students applying to non-screening majors are also encouraged to complete the California General Education Transfer Curriculum (Cal-GETC) or all UC Santa Cruz general education requirements prior to transfer. For guidance on incorporating Cal-GETC into your academic plan, consult your community college counselor.

notes.content: Some Advanced Placement (AP), International Baccalaureate Higher Level (IBH), and Advanced Level (A-level) exams satisfy UCSC course requirements. UCSC's exam credit policies may be found on the Degree Progress Unit website .
notes.contentHtml: Some Advanced Placement (AP), International Baccalaureate Higher Level (IBH), and Advanced Level (A-level) exams satisfy UCSC course requirements. UCSC's exam credit policies may be found on the Degree Progress Unit website.

notes.content: PREPARATION FOR THE MAJOR Although not required for admission, transfer students are encouraged to complete the following courses, or their articulated equivalents, prior to transferring: LING 50: Introduction to Linguistics LING 53: Semantics I Transfer students are also strongly encouraged to complete as much preparation as possible in the foreign language of their choosing. Students pursuing the concentration in Japanese or Chinese should place into Level 4 (Level 5 for students entering in the winter term) or higher when they enter UC Santa Cruz, if they wish to complete the major in two years. Students in other languages may be able to complete the major in two years if they have completed their general education requirements prior to transfer. Transfer students admitted to UC Santa Cruz for the winter term who do not place into Level 2 or higher in a language placement test when they matriculate may not be able to complete the major in a timely manner. Effective the 2025–2026 academic year, UCSC no longer offers German or Portuguese language courses. Because this major requires intermediate-high proficiency in a non-English language (equivalent to two years of UCSC coursework), students who began these languages at their community college will be unable to continue them at UCSC. Students are encouraged to complete the California General Education Transfer Curriculum (Cal-GETC) or all UC Santa Cruz general education requirements before transferring. For guidance on incorporating Cal-GETC into your academic plan, consult your community college counselor. Please visit the Linguistics website to learn more about this major. UC Santa Cruz General Catalog: Language Studies B.A. Program Statement
notes.contentHtml:
PREPARATION FOR THE MAJOR

Although not required for admission, transfer students are encouraged to complete the following courses, or their articulated equivalents, prior to transferring:


  • LING 50: Introduction to Linguistics
  • LING 53: Semantics I


Transfer students are also strongly encouraged to complete as much preparation as possible in the foreign language of their choosing. Students pursuing the concentration in Japanese or Chinese should place into Level 4 (Level 5 for students entering in the winter term) or higher when they enter UC Santa Cruz, if they wish to complete the major in two years. Students in other languages may be able to complete the major in two years if they have completed their general education requirements prior to transfer. Transfer students admitted to UC Santa Cruz for the winter term who do not place into Level 2 or higher in a language placement test when they matriculate may not be able to complete the major in a timely manner.


Effective the 2025–2026 academic year, UCSC no longer offers German or Portuguese language courses. Because this major requires intermediate-high proficiency in a non-English language (equivalent to two years of UCSC coursework), students who began these languages at their community college will be unable to continue them at UCSC.


Students are encouraged to complete the California General Education Transfer Curriculum (Cal-GETC) or all UC Santa Cruz general education requirements before transferring. For guidance on incorporating Cal-GETC into your academic plan, consult your community college counselor.



Please visit the Linguistics website to learn more about this major.

UC Santa Cruz General Catalog: Language Studies B.A. Program Statement
